# d3vsh0p

> Open source AI code orchestration — a Node.js state machine that turns product specs into tested, reviewed, and merged production code using Claude agents in parallel git worktrees.

Built by Chelsea Kent (@clkent). Licensed under BSL 1.1.

## What This Repo Is

d3vsh0p is a deterministic orchestrator, not a framework or library. It reads a structured roadmap, dispatches Claude agents to isolated git worktrees, runs them through implement → test → review → merge microcycles, and consolidates everything to main via PR. Agents are stateless — invoked fresh each time via the Anthropic Agent SDK with only the context they need.

## Project Structure

```
platform/orchestrator/          — The brain. Node.js state machine that drives everything
  src/index.js                  — CLI entry point (devshop command)
  src/microcycle.js             — Core loop: implement → test → review → merge per item
  src/parallel-orchestrator.js  — Runs groups concurrently in parallel worktrees
  src/session/state-machine.js  — State transitions for the orchestrator
  src/agents/                   — Agent pool, runner, session, and template engine
  src/commands/                 — CLI commands: kickoff, run, plan, pair, recover, etc.
  src/git/                      — Git operations, merge mutex, recovery manager
  src/quality/                  — Import verification, convention checks, health checks, review parsing
  src/runners/                  — Triage, repair, security, scaffolding, interventions
  src/session/                  — Cost tracking, state machine, path utils
  src/api/                      — REST API server (optional)

templates/agents/               — System prompt templates for each agent role
  implementation-agent/         — Developer agents (Jordan, Alex, Sam, Taylor)
  pm-agent/                     — Riley — creates specs and roadmaps from requirements
  principal-engineer/           — Morgan — reviews code, approves/rejects, investigates spikes
  security-agent/               — Casey — vulnerability scanning and security audits
  triage-agent/                 — Drew — classifies parked items as blocking/non-blocking
  spike-agent/                  — Morgan (spike mode) — investigates technical unknowns
  _shared/                      — Shared prompt fragments included across agents

openspec/                       — d3vsh0p's own specs (it uses OpenSpec on itself)
active-agents/                  — Per-project runtime state and session logs
templates/project-starter/      — Starter code copied when creating new projects
project-registry.example.json   — Example of the project index
```

## Key Concepts

### The Microcycle
Every roadmap item runs through: Select Item → Implement → Verify Imports → Test → Commit → Convention Check → Review → Merge. Failed items retry (capped), then go to a parking lot for human review.

### Phases and Groups
The roadmap is organized into phases (sequential, with dependencies) containing groups (concurrent). Each group gets its own git worktree and a randomly assigned agent from the pool.

### Agent Roles
- **Riley (PM)** — Creates OpenSpec specs and roadmap from brain dumps
- **Developer agents (x4)** — Jordan, Alex, Sam, Taylor — write code in isolated worktrees
- **Morgan (Principal Engineer)** — Reviews diffs, approves/rejects, investigates spikes, repairs broken projects
- **Casey (Security)** — Scans for vulnerabilities
- **Drew (Triage)** — Classifies parked items as blocking vs non-blocking

### State Machine
The orchestrator is a state machine (`src/session/state-machine.js`) with states like SELECTING_REQUIREMENT, IMPLEMENTING, TESTING, REVIEWING, MERGING, PROJECT_REPAIR, etc. All state is persisted to `state.json` — agents themselves are stateless.

### Git Strategy
- Session branches (`devshop/session-{timestamp}`) accumulate approved work
- Work branches (`devshop/work-{timestamp}/{requirement-id}`) are per-requirement
- Worktree branches (`devshop/worktree-{sessionId}/group-{letter}`) are per-group, temporary
- Merges are serialized through an async mutex (`src/git/merge-lock.js`)

## Key Files to Read First

1. `platform/orchestrator/src/microcycle.js` — The core loop, understand this and you understand d3vsh0p
2. `platform/orchestrator/src/parallel-orchestrator.js` — How groups run concurrently
3. `platform/orchestrator/src/session/state-machine.js` — All orchestrator state transitions
4. `platform/orchestrator/src/agents/agent-runner.js` — How agents are invoked via the Claude Agent SDK
5. `platform/orchestrator/src/agents/template-engine.js` — How system prompts are rendered per-agent
6. `platform/orchestrator/src/git/git-ops.js` — Worktree management, branching, merging
7. `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/run.js` — Entry point for `./devshop run`
8. `templates/agents/implementation-agent/` — What the developer agents see
9. `CLAUDE.md` — Instructions for AI assistants working on this codebase

## How to Modify Agent Behavior

Agent prompts live in `templates/agents/<role>/`. Each has a system prompt template with Handlebars-style variables (`{{projectName}}`, `{{specContent}}`, etc.) that the template engine fills at runtime. To change what an agent does, edit its system prompt. To change what context it receives, edit `src/agents/template-engine.js`.

## How to Add a New Command

1. Create `platform/orchestrator/src/commands/<name>.js`
2. Export an async function that receives the parsed CLI args
3. Register it in `platform/orchestrator/src/index.js`
4. Follow the OpenSpec workflow for non-trivial changes (see CLAUDE.md)

## Testing

```bash
cd platform/orchestrator && npm test
```

Tests are colocated with source files (`*.test.js`). Integration tests are suffixed `.integration.test.js`. A pre-commit hook runs tests automatically when orchestrator files change.
